title: Slippage & Execution Cost Model for Backtester
summary: Add a realistic per-trade slippage model to the backtester based on average daily volume (ADV). Most retail algo backtests overstate performance by 0.3-0.8% per round-trip because they assume fills at the close price with no market impact. A proper slippage model calibrates fill degradation to trade size as a fraction of ADV — available for free via yfinance. This doesn't improve win rate directly but prevents curve-fitting to unrealistic backtest results.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — Execution Quality & Exit Optimization
deferral_reason: Future; relevant at live trading stage — deferred during paper_trading_single_account prioritisation
